title: African American Women's Marital Attitudes: A Qualitative Study on Never Married Women
abstract: Over the past few decades, marriage rates in the United States among African Americans continue to decline, yet African American women continue to express a desire to be married. Using a grounded theory qualitative approach with semi-structured interviews (N = 23), we explored marital attitudes among never married African American women. Participants identified both negative and positive exposures to marriage during childhood and messaging from family and faith communities as major sources influencing their desire to marry. Other themes, such as respectability and child behavioral benefits emerged that influenced women's attitudes toward marriage. We present a discussion of the findings, limitations of this study, and next steps in the research.
authors_text: DeLoach McCutcheon, Kendra P.; Watkins, Karen Y.; Burton, Eboni V.; Harris, Arlaina C.
